title: from_uri() unit tests for K8sBackend
description:
What: Write exhaustive unit tests for K8sBackend::from_uri() covering valid URIs, invalid URIs, and edge cases. No credentials or network required.

Algorithm:
1. Add a #[cfg(test)] mod tests block to secretx-k8s/src/lib.rs (or a separate tests module).
2. Implement the 7 test cases listed in the W1A bead.
3. Add additional edge cases:
   - test_from_uri_key_empty_string: "secretx:k8s:default/name?key=" -> key==Some("") or InvalidUri (document decision)
   - test_from_uri_namespace_with_special_chars: k8s namespaces are DNS labels; consider whether to validate at parse time or leave to API error
4. Each test uses #[test] (not #[tokio::test]) since from_uri is synchronous.
5. Error assertions must check the variant, not just is_err(): assert!(matches!(result, Err(SecretError::InvalidUri(_)))).

Defensive programming rules:
- Tests must not print or log the URI path components as secret material.
- Each test must assert the specific error variant returned, not just that an error occurred.
- No network calls permitted in this test module.

Subagent instructions: Single subagent. Read the existing test patterns in secretx-gcp-sm/src/lib.rs before writing tests to match the project style. Run cargo test -p secretx-k8s after writing.

Tests — each is its own #[test] function:
| Test name | Condition | Oracle | Assertion |
|-----------|-----------|--------|-----------|
| from_uri_valid_no_key | valid URI, no key param | URI components themselves | namespace=="default", name=="my-secret", key==None |
| from_uri_valid_with_key | valid URI with ?key=password | URI components | key==Some("password") |
| from_uri_wrong_backend | aws-sm URI | expected variant | matches!(r, Err(SecretError::InvalidUri(_))) |
| from_uri_empty_namespace | /name path | expected variant | Err(InvalidUri) |
| from_uri_empty_name | ns/ path | expected variant | Err(InvalidUri) |
| from_uri_no_slash | no / in path | expected variant | Err(InvalidUri) |
| from_uri_slash_in_name | ns/a/b path | expected variant | Err(InvalidUri) |
